title: Respiratory picornavirus genotyping conventional nested RT-PCR ("Wisdom VP42 assay")
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"31ucp","text":"This is my preferred, previously published [Ref 1], rhinovirus (RV) and enterovirus (EV) genotyping assay when seeking to identify the genotype of a respiratory picornavirus detected in a clinical sample extract. It is employed after use of a screening real-time RT-PCR has identified a respiratory picornavirus.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"73mr9","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8j0ht","text":"I have not confirmed that it can detect every single RV genotype but I do know that it detects many from each of the three RV species (Human rhinovirus A, Human rhinovirus B and Human rhinovirus C) as well as at least some Human enterovirus (EV) genotypes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":95,"length":4},{"style":"italic","offset":135,"length":18},{"style":"italic","offset":155,"length":18},{"style":"italic","offset":178,"length":18},{"style":"italic","offset":223,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"25nar","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dt1iu","text":"The assay picks up EVs due to the shared genetic similarities in the 5'UTR target region. EVs can be discriminated using subgenomic sequencing (see  VP42 typing assay protocol), or simply described as 'respiratory EVs' since there is no specific-specific vaccine or treatment available anyway. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c5pkm","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"92js2","text":"This is a robust primary subgenomic sequencing assay. It is more sensitive than any VP1 protocols because it targets more conserved primer target sites. It produces a more reliable typing result than does the 5'UTR region alone. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7fd4n","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2633e","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"26eb4","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"3t3ea","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"imageblock","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"6A0DA326C3DE4EE088FF537E10B9F507","height":null,"is_video":false,"mime":null,"original_name":"HRV-genome_PRIMERS.png","placeholder":"https://content.protocols.io/u6kdyxe.png","source":"https://content.protocols.io/u6kdyxe.png","width":null}}}}

steps: SuperScript III One-Step RT-PCR System with Platinum Taq Invitrogen - Thermo Fisher Catalog #12457-026 MyFi Mix Bioline Catalog #BIO-25049

ROUND 1 REACTION MIX    Ideally, set up more reaction mixes than you'll need for both rounds at the same time Strips of 8x 0.2ml tubes are good for this use Freeze in a frost-free freezer at -20°C until needed %s %s RT-PCR (Round 1) Reagent Vol (μl) 1x Final reaction concentration Nuclease-free water 1.4 N/A SensiFAST no ROX One-Step Mix (2X) 10 1X Primers (μM)1 6 600nM MgCl2 0 3mM RNase inhibitor 0.4 Unknown RT/Taq (?U/μl) 0.2 Unknown Template 2 N/A Dispense 15µL to each reaction tube Total reaction volume will be 20µl %s %s PCR (Round 2) Reagent Vol (μl) 1x Final reaction concentration Nuclease-free water 8.7 N/A MyTaq Reaction Buffer (5X) 4 1X Primers (μM)1 3.8 380nM MgCl2 1.4 4.75mM MyTaq HS DNA Polymerase (5U/uL) 0.2 Unknown 1st round amplicon 2 N/A Dispense 18µL into each reaction tube Total reaction volume will be 20µl

This reaction has been run using a SimpliAmp (Applied Biosystems) thermal cycler Transfer 5µl of nucleic acid extract (extracted RNA, controls or NTC [nuclease-free water]) to defrosted reaction tubes and cycle %s %s RT-PCR (Round 1) 45°C 20 min 1X 94°C 2 min 1X 94°C 18 sec | 50°C 21 sec | 35X 72°C 90 sec | 72°C 7 min 1X 4°C ∞
This reaction has been run using a SimpliAmp (Applied Biosystems) thermal cycler Transfer 2µl of 1:100 pre-diluted Round 1 amplicon into defrosted 0.2ml reaction tubes and cycle %s %s 95°C1 min1X
electrophorese 2-5µl amplicon on a 1.5% agarose gel in 0.5X TBE buffer
creator: Ian M Mackay
